title: Verbascum zaianense
intro: Verbascum zaianense, also known as Zaian mullein, is a perennial herbaceous plant belonging to the Scrophulariaceae family, characterized by its tall stature and woolly leaves. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, providing relief from respiratory issues, such as coughs and colds, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory effects, which also contribute to its potential in treating skin conditions and wounds. The bioactive constituents of Verbascum zaianense include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made from Zaian mullein include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to support respiratory health and alleviate various inflammatory conditions.
taxonomy: Verbascum zaianense is classified within the family Scrophulariaceae, a family of flowering plants commonly known as figworts. This plant is further classified as a member of the genus Verbascum, which includes about 200 species of flowering plants. The genus Verbascum is part of the order Lamiales, a large and diverse group of flowering plants. The Scrophulariaceae family, to which Verbascum zaianense belongs, is part of the larger clade of flowering plants, the angiosperms. This classification helps to categorize Verbascum zaianense and other related species within the plant kingdom.
